options_notice.title: Finish setup in Options
options_notice.description:
The thermostat "{name}" has been configured with sensor **{sensor}**.

Open the integration options after creation, add at least one **heater** or **cooler**, review the thermostat settings, then save.

error.invalid_temp_range: Invalid temperature range: the **minimum** must be lower than the **maximum**.
error.preset_target_required: At least one **target temperature** is required.
error.already_configured: A thermostat with this **name** and **temperature sensor** is already configured.

init.title: Universal Thermostat overview

data_description.min_temp: Lowest target temperature the thermostat will allow to set
data_description.max_temp: Highest target temperature the thermostat will allow to set
data_description.auto_cool_delta: In `auto` mode, coolers use the thermostat target plus this delta. Example: target 22 and delta 1.0 gives a cooling target of 23
data_description.auto_heat_delta: In `auto` mode, heaters use the thermostat target minus this delta. Example: target 22 and delta 1.0 gives a heating target of 21

controllers_menu.title: Controllers

controller_add.title: Add heater or cooler
controller_add.description: Choose the entity to control and whether it should heat or cool. The same physical device can be added as a **heater** and as a **cooler**, for example an A/C unit with both modes.

controller_mode.title: Add {controller_type}
controller_mode.description:
Choose how Universal Thermostat should control this entity.

{current_entity_id}

data_description.inverted: Use when the entity's `on` and `off` behavior is reversed. For a switch, Universal Thermostat will call `turn_off` to start and `turn_on` to stop.
data_description.ignore_windows: Keep this controller running even when a configured window is open.
data_description.keep_alive: Repeat the current command on this interval. Useful for devices that stop unless they periodically receive a command.
data_description.cold_tolerance: For heaters, how far below the target temperature the room must fall before heating starts. For coolers, how far below the target temperature the room must fall before cooling stops. Example: target 21.0 and tolerance 0.3 starts heating at 20.7.
data_description.hot_tolerance: For coolers, how far above the target temperature the room must rise before cooling starts. For heaters, how far above the target temperature the room must rise before heating stops. Example: target 24.0 and tolerance 0.3 starts cooling at 24.3.
data_description.target_temp_delta: Heating sends target plus this delta; cooling sends target minus this delta. Example: target 21 and delta 1 sends 22 for heating or 20 for cooling.
data_description.min_cycle_duration: Minimum time the controller must stay in its current state before Universal Thermostat changes it again. Helps prevent rapid on/off cycling.
data_description.kp: How strongly PID reacts to the current temperature error. Increase for a stronger immediate response.
data_description.ki: How strongly PID reacts to accumulated error over time. Increase carefully if the temperature settles too far from the target.
data_description.kd: How strongly PID reacts to fast temperature changes. Increase carefully to reduce overshoot.
data_description.pid_sample_period: How often PID recalculates the output. Leave empty to recalculate on every relevant update.
data_description.pwm_period: Length of one PWM cycle. A PID output of 25% keeps the switch on for 25% of each period and off for the rest.
data_description.min: Lower limit for PID output. For number entities, this is clamped to the entity's minimum value.
data_description.max: Upper limit for PID output. For number entities, this is clamped to the entity's maximum value.
data_description.switch_entity_id: Optional switch used with a number PID controller. Universal Thermostat turns it on while the number output is active and off when control stops.
data_description.switch_inverted: Use when the optional switch entity has reversed logic.
data_description.clear_keep_alive: Use to remove the saved keep-alive interval.
data_description.clear_target_temp_delta: Use to remove the saved target temperature delta.
data_description.clear_min_cycle_duration: Use to remove the saved minimum cycle duration.
data_description.clear_pid_sample_period: Use to remove the saved PID sample period.
data_description.clear_min: Use to remove the saved minimum PID output.
data_description.clear_max: Use to remove the saved maximum PID output.
data_description.clear_switch_entity_id: Use to remove the saved switch entity.

controller_remove.title: Remove heater or cooler
controller_remove.description: Choose the controller to remove from this thermostat.

windows_menu.title: Windows

window_add.title: Add window
window_add.description: Choose and set up a binary entity.

data_description.inverted: Use when `off` should mean open or active, for example some presence sensors.
data_description.timeout: Wait this long after the **window** changes before stopping or restarting controllers.

data_description.inverted: Use when `off` should mean open or active, for example some presence sensors.
data_description.timeout: Wait this long after the **window** changes before stopping or restarting controllers.
data_description.clear_timeout: Enable to remove the saved delay.

presets_menu.title: Presets

preset_add.title: Add preset
preset_add.description:
Choose a **preset** and how it should change temperatures.

**Single temperature delta** shifts the current target temperature. In `heat/cool` mode it shifts both low and high targets.

**Heating/cooling temperature deltas** shift heating and cooling targets separately. In `auto` mode, they adjust the automatic heating/cooling targets.

**Target temperatures** set fixed target temperatures.

preset_config.title: Configure preset
preset_config.description:
Configure preset **{current_preset}**.

Only the fields for the selected preset type are shown.

data_description.temp_delta: Adds this value to the thermostat target temperature. Example: target 21 and delta -2 changes the preset target to 19.
data_description.heat_delta: Adds this value to the heating target. In `heat/cool` mode it changes the low target. In `auto` mode it adjusts the automatic heating target.
data_description.cool_delta: Adds this value to the cooling target. In `heat/cool` mode it changes the high target. In `auto` mode it adjusts the automatic cooling target.
data_description.target_temp: Sets one fixed target temperature when heating/cooling-specific targets are not configured.
data_description.clear_target_temp: Enable to remove the saved single target temperature.
data_description.heat_target_temp: Sets a fixed heating target. In `heat/cool` mode this is the low target. In `auto` mode it is used only when a cooling target is also set.
data_description.clear_heat_target_temp: Enable to remove the saved heating target temperature.
data_description.cool_target_temp: Sets a fixed cooling target. In `heat/cool` mode this is the high target. In `auto` mode it is used only when a heating target is also set.
data_description.clear_cool_target_temp: Enable to remove the saved cooling target temperature.

error.invalid_temp_range: Invalid temperature range: the minimum must be lower than the maximum.
error.preset_exists: A preset with this name already exists.
error.preset_target_required: At least one target temperature is required.
